title: Cons & Courtroom
subtitle: Long cons, courtroom thrillers, twist-driven films. Plot architecture as the primary pleasure.

sections.name: I. Cross-Listed (Already in Other Tabs)
sections.desc: Films you've already loved that fit this lane. Listed for completeness.

items.title: Witness for the Prosecution
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Classics. Marked Loved + 'all-time favorite' per your record. The structural template for everything in this tab.

items.title: Catch Me If You Can
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Heist. Marked Loved per your record.

items.title: Spy Game
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Spy. Marked Loved + 'all-time favorite'. The flashback-structure-as-debrief device is a perfect example of plot architecture as primary pleasure.

items.title: All the President's Men
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Spy. Marked Loved per your record.

items.title: The Departed
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Crime. Marked Loved per your record.

sections.name: II. High Priority — Sorkin Films
sections.desc: Sorkin is now a confirmed pillar. These are his major film credits, all in this lane.

items.title: A Few Good Men
items.whyPriority: Sorkin's screenplay (adapted from his play). Cruise/Nicholson/Moore. Military courtroom thriller. 'You can't handle the truth.' Direct fit for everything Sorkin landed with you.
items.pitch: Two Marines are charged with a fellow Marine's death. Their lawyer must determine whether they were ordered to harm him.

critics.quote: Four Oscar nominations including Best Picture. Nicholson Best Supporting Actor nomination.

items.title: The Social Network
items.whyPriority: Sorkin + Fincher. Two depositions framing a flashback structure. Won 3 Oscars including Best Adapted Screenplay. Direct fit for plot-architecture-as-primary-pleasure.
items.pitch: Mark Zuckerberg founds Facebook. Two simultaneous lawsuits surround him. The film cuts between them and the events they're litigating.

critics.quote: Won 3 Oscars. Eight nominations including Best Picture. Considered one of the films most often cited as 'should have won Best Picture.'

items.title: Molly's Game
items.whyPriority: Sorkin's directorial debut. Jessica Chastain runs an underground high-stakes poker ring; the FBI catches her. Idris Elba as her lawyer. Long-con + courtroom + Sorkin.
items.pitch: Olympic skier becomes runner of the world's most exclusive private poker games. The IRS and FBI come for her. True story.

critics.quote: Sorkin Oscar nomination for Adapted Screenplay. Critically warm.

items.title: Charlie Wilson's War
items.whyPriority: Sorkin + Mike Nichols. Hanks, Hoffman, Roberts. True story of the Texas congressman who armed the Afghan mujahideen against the Soviets. Spy + politics + Sorkin dialogue.
items.pitch: A Texas congressman, a Houston socialite, and a CIA officer covertly funnel arms to the Afghan resistance. Hanks/Hoffman/Roberts.

critics.quote: Hoffman Best Supporting Actor nomination. Sorkin's screenplay among his sharpest.

sections.name: III. High Priority — Twist Architecture
sections.desc: Films where the structural payoff is the point. Direct fits for what's worked already.

items.title: The Sting
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed in Heist. The con-artist film template. Newman + Redford. Won 7 Oscars including Best Picture. The reveal structure is impeccable.
items.pitch: Two grifters set up an elaborate revenge against a mob boss who killed their friend.

critics.quote: Won 7 Oscars including Best Picture, Director, Original Screenplay.

items.title: The Usual Suspects
items.whyPriority: The reveal-driven thriller template for the modern era. Spacey won Supporting Actor; Christopher McQuarrie won Original Screenplay. Direct fit.
items.pitch: A small-time con man tells a prosecutor about a heist gone wrong. The 'Keyser Söze' reveal is one of cinema's most famous.

critics.quote: Won 2 Oscars. Aged into a different reception due to Singer's reputation; the film's craft remains undisputed.

items.title: Inside Man
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Heist. Bank heist that's actually about something else entirely. The architecture of the deception is the appeal.

items.title: Memento
items.whyPriority: Nolan's structural breakthrough. Two parallel narratives — one running forward, one running backward — meet in the middle. Direct fit for your Smart Structure pillar.
items.pitch: A man with no short-term memory hunts the person who killed his wife. Tattoos and Polaroids are his memory.

critics.quote: Two Oscar nominations including Best Original Screenplay. Established Nolan.

items.title: Gone Girl
items.whyPriority: Fincher again. Mid-film perspective shift is one of the great structural moves. Pike Oscar-nominated. Flynn adapted her own novel.
items.pitch: A wife disappears on her wedding anniversary. The husband becomes the prime suspect. Then the film changes.

critics.quote: Pike Best Actress nomination. Considered Fincher's most accessible major film.

items.title: 12 Angry Men
items.whyPriority: The single-room courtroom-jury film. Henry Fonda anchors. Lumet's debut. The genre-defining example of constraint producing depth.
items.pitch: Twelve jurors deliberate a murder case in real time. One man initially holds out for not guilty.

critics.quote: Three Oscar nominations. AFI Top-100. Routinely cited among the greatest American films.

sections.name: IV. High Priority — Courtroom
sections.desc: Direct heir to Witness for the Prosecution.

items.title: Anatomy of a Murder
items.whyPriority: James Stewart at peak. The first Hollywood film to use the words 'rape,' 'panties,' 'sperm.' Procedural courtroom drama at its most rigorous.
items.pitch: A small-town Michigan lawyer defends an Army officer who killed the man who allegedly raped his wife.

critics.quote: Seven Oscar nominations including Best Picture. Routinely cited as one of the greatest courtroom films.

items.title: Anatomy of a Fall
items.whyPriority: Won Palme d'Or 2023. French. Sandra Hüller as a writer accused of murdering her husband. The trial is the film. Direct fit.
items.pitch: A man falls from a chalet window in the French Alps. Was it suicide? An accident? Or did his wife push him? The trial unpacks their marriage.

critics.quote: Palme d'Or. Won Best Original Screenplay at the 2024 Oscars.

items.title: Presumed Innocent
items.whyPriority: Pakula again — same director as All The President's Men. Harrison Ford as a prosecutor accused of murdering his colleague-and-lover. Twist ending.
items.pitch: A married prosecutor is assigned to investigate the murder of his former lover. He becomes the prime suspect.

critics.quote: Considered one of the great 90s adult thrillers. Scott Turow novel adaptation.

items.title: The Verdict
items.whyPriority: Paul Newman as a washed-up Boston lawyer. David Mamet screenplay. Five Oscar nominations. Quiet, devastating, one of Newman's career-best performances.
items.pitch: A failing alcoholic Boston lawyer takes a medical malpractice case he expects to settle. He decides to go to trial instead.

critics.quote: Five Oscar nominations including Best Picture, Director, Actor (Newman), Adapted Screenplay (Mamet).

items.title: The Lincoln Lawyer
items.whyPriority: Michael Connelly novel adaptation. McConaughey as a defense lawyer who works out of his Lincoln Town Car. Sharp, structurally tight, 90s-throwback feel.
items.pitch: A defense lawyer takes on a wealthy client accused of attempted rape. The case isn't what it seems.

critics.quote: Critical and commercial hit. Spawned the 2022 Netflix series.

sections.name: V. Medium Priority — More Twists & Cons

items.title: Body Heat
items.whyPriority: Kasdan's directorial debut. Florida noir, William Hurt and Kathleen Turner. Femme-fatale-driven plot architecture.
items.pitch: Florida lawyer is seduced by a wealthy man's wife. They plan to murder him.

critics.quote: Considered the great neo-noir of the 1980s. Career-launcher for Kasdan.

items.title: Sleuth (1972)
items.whyPriority: Two-hander with Caine and Olivier. Single location. Both nominated for Best Actor for the same film. Game-of-cat-and-mouse twist structure.
items.pitch: A wealthy English mystery novelist invites his wife's lover to his country house. He has plans.

critics.quote: Both Caine and Olivier received Best Actor nominations. Anthony Shaffer adapted his own play.

items.title: Mulholland Drive
items.whyPriority: Cross-listed in Auteur (Lynch). Most accessible Lynch despite being his most famously confusing. The structural reveal — the dreaming protagonist — is genuinely puzzle-shaped.
items.pitch: An aspiring actress arrives in LA and meets a woman with amnesia. The film is more than it appears.

critics.quote: Voted #8 Greatest Film of All Time in critics poll. Routinely cited as the greatest film of the 21st century.

items.title: Jagged Edge
items.whyPriority: Underrated 80s legal thriller. Glenn Close defends a publisher accused of murdering his wife. Eszterhas screenplay. Twist ending.
items.pitch: A retired prosecutor takes one more case as a defense attorney. She begins a relationship with her client. Things complicate.

critics.quote: Two Oscar nominations. Established the template for many 80s/90s legal thrillers.

items.title: The Talented Mr. Ripley
items.whyPriority: Patricia Highsmith adaptation. Damon as Ripley. Long con as character study. The film's elegance is its core asset.
items.pitch: A young man is paid to bring a wealthy heir back from Italy. He decides he'd rather become him.

critics.quote: Five Oscar nominations including Adapted Screenplay (Minghella) and Supporting Actor (Law).

items.title: American Hustle
items.pitch: Cross-listed in Heist. Marked Queued per your record.

items.title: Primal Fear
items.whyPriority: Edward Norton's debut. Defense attorney (Gere) takes on the case of an altar boy accused of murdering an archbishop. Late-90s legal thriller with a strong twist.
items.pitch: A Chicago defense attorney takes on a high-profile murder case. The accused is a stuttering teenage altar boy.

critics.quote: Norton received an Oscar nomination for the role. Critical hit.

items.title: Glengarry Glen Ross
items.whyPriority: Mamet adapting his own play. Closest film cousin to a courtroom film without being one — every scene is structured argument. Pacino, Lemmon, Baldwin, Spacey, Harris, Arkin.
items.pitch: Real estate salesmen at a Chicago office in two impossible nights. Always be closing.

critics.quote: Mamet's screenplay considered among the most influential of the modern era. Lemmon Volpi Cup at Venice.

items.title: Witness for the Prosecution
items.pitch: Cross-listed Classics. Already loved.

items.title: Inherit the Wind
items.pitch: Spencer Tracy as Clarence Darrow analog defending a teacher charged with teaching evolution. The Scopes Trial fictionalized.

critics.quote: Four Oscar nominations. Considered one of the great courtroom films.

items.title: Judgment at Nuremberg
items.pitch: Spencer Tracy presides over the trial of Nazi judges. Three-hour epic courtroom drama.

critics.quote: Won 2 Oscars. Eleven nominations including Best Picture, Best Actor (Tracy).

items.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
items.pitch: Gregory Peck as Atticus Finch. The defining Hollywood courtroom film of the era.

critics.quote: Won 3 Oscars. Eight nominations including Best Picture.
